Maya: Welcome back to Singletrack Sessions. Today we're talking about enduro racing, and I'm joined by Sophie Lorenz, enduro racer and coach. Sophie Lorenz, thanks for being here.
Sophie Lorenz: Thanks, Maya. Great to be back.
Maya: Today's episode is sponsored by Linear. Linear is the issue tracker built for speed — keyboard-first. Try linear.com today.Before we get into it — Liam Verbeck was on the show talking about how drainage is the single highest-leverage design choice.
Sophie Lorenz: Yeah, exactly. And it ties into what we're covering today.
Maya: I want to flag something — Liam Verbeck mentioned this same dynamic a couple episodes back.Walk me through a concrete example. Where do soil erosion and enduro racing intersect?
Sophie Lorenz: Honestly, Speed comes from braking earlier and smoother, not from taking bigger risks. We've seen this play out at teams using RockShox. I've watched this pattern hold across three different teams.
Maya: I want to dig into that. Where do soil erosion and enduro racing intersect?
Sophie Lorenz: Sure. Tire pressure and casing choice often matter more than a minor suspension tweak. The framing matters because it tells you which trade-offs you're making.
Maya: Let's start there. Where do soil erosion and enduro racing intersect?
Sophie Lorenz: Honestly, Risk management in racing isn't avoiding crashes — it's choosing which crashes are survivable. We've seen this play out at teams using Shimano. If you ignore that, you end up rebuilding it from scratch a year later.
Maya: Where does that break down?
Sophie Lorenz: Cascadia Alliance is one of the platforms where this shows up cleanly. And the second-order effect is the part most people miss.
Maya: What's the one heuristic you keep coming back to? How does Spoke & Wrench fit into this picture?
Sophie Lorenz: Here's how I think about it. Erosion patterns on race-tracks change line choice from practice to race day. Once you internalize that, the rest of the decisions get easier.
Maya: Before we keep going — head over to our sponsor's website if you want to support the show. Strava is the home for athletes.We'll be right back after a quick word from our sponsors.
